summary: Receive task, assess complexity, select pipeline tier, check expertise, resolve profiles, delegate to specialists.

content.description: Sizes incoming tasks and selects the appropriate execution pipeline. Resolves abstract pipeline functions to concrete specialist profiles.

steps.name: Assess
steps.instruction: Evaluate: Direct mode (clear, bounded) or Alignment mode (ambiguous, architectural). If alignment needed, present approach and wait.

steps.name: Size
steps.instruction: Determine pipeline tier: Small (1 file, bug fix) → execute only. Medium (multi-file, feature slice) → execute + review. Large (new feature, architecture) → analyze + execute + test + review. Security-sensitive → add security review to any tier.

steps.name: Pre-execution check
steps.instruction: Evaluate expertise: domain, specialist availability, confidence level. If low confidence, escalate before proceeding.

steps.name: Resolve profiles
steps.instruction: Map abstract pipeline functions (execute, review-code, analyze-architecture) to concrete profiles from .leo/profiles/ based on project context.

steps.name: Delegate
steps.instruction: Fire specialists in sequence per the pipeline. Each specialist uses the model defined in config (default: sonnet).

do_not: Execute the task directly — always delegate
Use Opus for specialist execution — specialists use Sonnet
Skip the pre-execution check for 'simple' tasks
